title: Demote baked default kinds to a jit-init-authored item_kinds table
description:
Today `jit init` writes no `[item_kinds]` table (main.rs:889-948), so the four built-in kinds come from `builtin_default_kinds` (item.rs:1194) via the `resolve_item_kinds` None-branch (item.rs:1229-1243). Baking domain kind names into the binary mildly violates the domain-agnostic principle.

Scope: have `jit init` emit an editable `[item_kinds]` table (the invariant entry using the generalized toml source descriptor); remove the baked `*_default` kind-name constants (item.rs:62-122,543-686) and `builtin_default_kinds` from the indexing path. Migrate THIS repo`s `.jit/config.toml` to carry the COMPLETE table in the same change: all former built-ins PLUS a dogfood `definition` kind over `docs/reference/glossary.md`, so no kind indexing regresses (a present table replaces built-ins verbatim). Update default-pinning tests (item.rs:1486-1552,1713-1728; harness_demo.rs:295; validation/graph.rs:1815; tests/decision_kind_tests.rs:10; tests/risk_kind_tests.rs:10; tests/cross_substrate_generality_tests.rs). The init-authored invariant entry uses the generalized toml source descriptor, and the indexing path contains no reserved-name branch.

## Success Criteria
- [hard] REQ-04: `jit init` emits an editable `[item_kinds]` table and a tree-wide grep (including tests, fixtures, and docs) finds no domain kind-name literal (requirement/decision/risk/invariant) in the indexing path; a repo with an explicit `[item_kinds]` table indexes with no built-ins (pinned by a golden init test), and this repo's config carries the complete table so no kind indexing regresses.
